analysis: TSLA is in a sustained downtrend from $485 to $372, making consistent lower highs and lower lows. The 3/19-3/20 sessions saw acceleration through $380 support on increasing volume (67M-78M), and the 3/26 close at $372 puts price at new 60-day lows. EMA/SMA structure fully bearish, MACD confirms, and the AD line diverges negatively from OBV, suggesting distribution beneath the surface. Put/call skew bearish at -7.0 aligns with directional thesis.
key_levels_rationale: 364.46 is the intraday low from 3/20 and the immediate downside magnet. 385-396 resistance zone is the 3/25 reaction high and the 3/6-3/16 consolidation range that should cap any bounces.

analysis: META dropped from $594 to $547 on 3/26 with massive volume (35.7M vs 12M avg), a climactic breakdown below the $593-604 support shelf that held since mid-March. The stock has been in a lower-high structure since the $738 January peak, and this volume-confirmed breakdown through a multi-week floor is a high-conviction continuation signal. EMA/SMA fully bearish, MACD bearish, sector RS deeply negative. The put/call skew is bullish (+7.0) which suggests options market may be leaning contrarian, but the tape breakdown overrides.
key_levels_rationale: 543.35 is the 3/26 intraday low and immediate support. 593-606 is the broken support shelf from mid-March which should now act as resistance. A gap-fill toward $530-520 is the measured move target.

analysis: ARM staged a massive breakout on 3/25, gapping from $135 to $157 on 29.5M shares (5x normal volume), driven by what appears to be a catalyst event. Price has held above the breakout level at $154.80 with strong EMA/SMA structure, bullish MACD, and top-tier sector relative strength. However, stochastic RSI is deeply overbought at -8.0 strength and AD line is bearish, warning that a pullback to retest the breakout zone is likely before continuation.
key_levels_rationale: 148.25 is the 3/25 gap-up open and key retest level for any pullback. 166.69 is the 3/25 intraday high and the immediate upside target. A hold above 148 on a pullback confirms the breakout.

analysis: NFLX made a dramatic V-bottom from $75.86 (2/12) to $99.17 (3/5), driven by a massive gap on 2/27 (200M+ volume). Since then, it has consolidated in a $90-99 range, and the 3/26 session showed a strong bounce from $91 to $93.32 with elevated volume (59M). Stochastic RSI bullish, AD line bullish, and Fibonacci support at the 0.382 retracement. However, MACD is bearish (-8.0) signaling momentum loss, and Bollinger bands are bearish, suggesting the consolidation could resolve lower.
key_levels_rationale: 90.69-91.01 is the 3/19-3/26 demand zone where buyers have stepped in twice. 99-100 is the March high cluster that represents the upside target. A break below 90 negates the bull thesis.

analysis: MSFT has been in a relentless downtrend from $487 to $366, losing over 25% with no meaningful bounce structure. The 3/24-3/26 sessions accelerated the selloff with price slicing through $372-383 on increasing volume. All trend indicators are aligned bearish: EMA/SMA, MACD (-8.0), sector RS (-10.0), VWAP, and Bollinger bands. OBV confirms distribution. The clean lower-high-lower-low structure with no base-building attempts makes this a high-conviction continuation short.
key_levels_rationale: 365.19 is the 3/26 intraday low. The $383-391 zone is the 3/23-3/18 resistance shelf. No visible accumulation pattern suggests further downside toward $350, the round-number psychological level.

analysis: DOCN has surged from $52 to $87 in a parabolic move over three weeks, with the 3/25 session printing 14M volume (10x average) on a wide-range candle. EMA/SMA bullish, MACD bullish, AD line positive, and options flow bullish. However, stochastic RSI is deeply overbought (-8.0 bearish), ATR is contracting, and volume surge is negative, signaling the momentum impulse may be exhausting. The 3/26 close at $85.62 below the 3/25 high of $88.25 shows initial selling pressure.
key_levels_rationale: 82.44-84.28 is the 3/24-3/26 demand zone. 88.25-88.84 is the double-top resistance from 3/23-3/25. A pullback to the $82 area offers a better risk/reward entry for continuation.

analysis: ORCL has collapsed from $204 to $142 in a persistent downtrend. A brief relief rally to $163 on 3/11 (83M volume spike likely earnings/news-driven) was immediately sold off, confirming the lower-high structure. MACD is near-max bearish (-9.3), EMA/SMA fully bearish, and put/call skew is bearish (-7.0), confirming institutional hedging. The 3/25-3/26 breakdown below $146 support is the latest leg lower, with price closing near the session lows.
key_levels_rationale: 142.10 (2/27 low) and 138.59 (2/23 low) are the next support levels. The $150-155 zone is the broken support shelf from mid-March acting as overhead resistance. The measured move from the failed $163 bounce targets $133.

analysis: APP has crashed from $730 to $391, a 46% decline characterized by extreme volatility and successive lower highs. The 3/26 session broke below the $430 support with a gap down to $412 open and closed at $391, the lowest level since the $387/$375 February lows. EMA/SMA and MACD are both at maximum bearish (-10.0). Despite OBV showing a bullish divergence (+7.0), the tape structure is overwhelmingly bearish with no basing pattern visible.
key_levels_rationale: 388.60 is the 3/26 intraday low and immediate support. 365-375 is the February low cluster. The $413-436 zone is the 3/25-3/26 gap resistance. The OBV divergence is notable but insufficient to override the dominant bearish structure.

analysis: MU peaked at $461 on 3/17-3/18 and has sold off violently to $355, losing 23% in nine sessions on expanding volume (54M on 3/26 vs 30M avg). The breakdown from the $400 level on 3/23 was decisive, and 3/25-3/26 sliced through $382 and $370 with no bounce. MACD is max bearish (-10.0), EMA/SMA bearish, Fibonacci retracement bearish. The Bollinger band and ATR readings suggest elevated volatility will persist.
key_levels_rationale: 350 is the 3/26 intraday low and a round-number support. 343-345 is the January consolidation zone. The broken $382-395 shelf now acts as overhead resistance. Momentum favors continuation to $335.

analysis: AAL has declined from $16 to $10.71, a 33% decline, but the pace of selling has decelerated in March with price consolidating in the $10.30-11.17 range. MACD is bullish (+10.0) signaling a momentum divergence against the bearish trend structure (EMA/SMA -10.0). OBV and AD line are both deeply bearish, confirming persistent distribution. The conflicting MACD divergence vs structural bearishness creates a mixed signal that limits conviction in either direction.
key_levels_rationale: 10.26-10.30 is the March low cluster. 11.17-11.44 is the overhead resistance from the early March bounce. The tight consolidation resembles a bear flag, but the MACD divergence warns of a possible failed breakdown.

analysis: DKNG has been in a sustained downtrend from $36 to $21, punctuated by a gap down on 2/13 from $25 to $21.76 on 65M volume. After a brief recovery attempt to $25.87 in early March, price has rolled over again, breaking below $23 on 3/25 with heavy volume (21M) and closing at $21.27 near the February gap levels. EMA/SMA and MACD are both at max bearish (-10.0). The failed bounce pattern and return to gap support increases the probability of a breakdown below $21.
key_levels_rationale: 20.89-21.01 is the February gap low zone that represents the last support before a measured move to $19. The $23.31-24.91 zone is the broken March support acting as resistance. A close below $20.89 triggers the next leg down.

analysis: ONON broke down sharply on 3/25-3/26, falling from $39.59 to $32.11 on massive volume (24.9M and 16.8M vs 4-5M average). This is a climactic breakdown through the $37-38 support zone that held through early March. EMA/SMA fully bearish, Bollinger bands bearish, VWAP bearish, and the volume surge confirms institutional selling. The two-day decline of 19% on 5x normal volume is a high-conviction distribution event with no visible demand.
key_levels_rationale: 32.05 is the 3/26 intraday low. The $34.95-37.49 zone is the broken 3/25 gap range that should now act as resistance. No historical price support is visible until the $28-30 zone, making the downside target achievable.
